preamble: The shift from the bilabial stop [b] to the labiodental (or bilabial) fricative [v] or approximant [β] is a classic instance of spirantization (lenition). It is especially common in intervocalic position, where the oral closure weakens. In Spanish, this merged with the reflex of Latin /v/ (originally [w]) to produce a single phoneme realized as [b] word-initially and [β] elsewhere.

examples.note: Intervocalic Latin /b/ spirantized to [β]; also illustrates betacism (merger of /b/ and /v/).
